Name: Women in HPC (WHPC)
Description: International community supporting women and underrepresented groups in HPC through mentoring, networking, leadership development, conferences, and career opportunities.

Name: Black in/HPC
Description: Community supporting Black and African American professionals in HPC. Annual conferences, networking events, and mentorship opportunities.

Name: CaRCC (Coalition for Academic Scientific Computation)
Description: Coalition advancing research computing and data careers through professionalization, workforce development, and community building. Career tracks, mentoring, and working groups.

Name: SGCI / SGX3 (Science Gateways Community Institute)
Description: Center of excellence for science gateways: consultation, gateways-as-a-service, education programs, workforce development, sustainability consultancy.

Name: ACCESS Campus Champions
Description: National network of research computing advocates helping researchers and students access ACCESS cyberinfrastructure resources, training, and technical expertise.

Name: Open HPC
Description: Industry consortium driving HPC infrastructure innovation through open-source collaboration. Provides standardized HPC distributions, interoperability specifications, and a community platform for vendors, researchers, and users to advance HPC systems and software.

Name: MS-CC (Minority Serving Institutions Cyberinfrastructure Consortium)
Description: Consortium supporting cyberinfrastructure capacity building at Minority Serving Institutions. Develops CS/IT programs and research infrastructure at HBCUs, MSIs, community colleges.

Name: PathwaysToScience.org
Description: Comprehensive hub connecting Indigenous, Black, Hispanic/Latinx, and LGBTQ+ students and professionals with STEM career resources, fellowships, conferences, and funding.

Name: NSBE (National Society of Black Engineers)
Description: The largest and most visible member-serving organization committed to the success and academic achievement of Black engineers. Offers mentorship, professional development, career fairs, and HPC/scholarship programs.

Name: SACNAS (Society for Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science)
Description: Leading STEM organization advancing Hispanic and Native American scientists. Provides mentorship, professional development, networking events, and scholarships at all career stages.

Name: Latinas in STEM
Description: Advancing Latinas in scientific and technical careers through mentoring, advocacy, scholarships, and professional development.

Name: SHPE (Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers)
Description: The world's largest resource for empowering the Latinx community to achieve their goals as engineers and scientists. Offers conferences, scholarships, career fairs, and professional development.

Name: ACM
Description: Association for Computing Machinery - the world's largest educational and scientific computing society. Student memberships, SIGs, and conferences.

Name: IEEE Computer Society
Description: IEEE Computer Society advancing computing theory and practice. Student memberships and specialized technical committees.

Name: US-RSE (Research Software Engineering Association)
Description: Professional organization for Scientific Computing and Research Software Engineering. Offers networking, professional development, job opportunities, and community building.

Name: ACCESS Careers
Description: ACCESS career board listing computing and cyberinfrastructure positions at academic and research institutions nationwide.

Name: CaRCC HR Job Family Matrix
Description: Framework for research computing and data career paths, including salary benchmarks and job family definitions.

Name: NRP (National Research Platform)
Description: Provides accessible cloud-based computational resources, data science tools, and computing infrastructure for researchers and students across all disciplines.

Name: Jupyter
Description: Interactive computing platform supporting notebooks, code, and data visualizations. Widely used in HPC environments for exploratory analysis, teaching, and reproducible research.

Name: Apptainer
Description: Open-source container platform designed for HPC and scientific computing. Successor to Singularity, enabling reproducible, portable computing environments for research workflows.

Name: ACCESS Cyberinfrastructure
Description: National platform providing access to supercomputers, data repositories, visualization resources, and software tools for the research community.

Name: NSF GRFP
Description: National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program for STEM students pursuing research-based careers.

Name: DOE CSGF
Description: Department of Energy Computational Science Graduate Fellowship for graduate students in computational science and related fields.

Name: ACCESS Workshops
Description: Workshops and training events covering HPC, data science, visualization, and cyberinfrastructure tools.

Name: NCSA Learn
Description: NCSA's free online learning portal featuring self-paced courses on HPC, Linux, Python, Git, CUDA, and research computing.

Name: BSSW - Formal Course Resources for Learning About HPC
Description: Argonne/Sandia-curated article compiling university courses and educational materials on high-performance computing.

Name: Intro to HPC - SLURM Training (Cambi)
Description: Free online self-study module covering SLURM workload manager basics - job submission, resource requests, batch scripting, squeue/sinfo/sbatch commands, and job arrays.

Name: HSF Training - Singularity Container Training
Description: High Energy Physics community's training material on Singularity containers for HPC. Covers container basics, workflows, data management, and security.

Name: The Carpentries - Adopting HPC Carpentry
Description: Carpentries blog post on strategies, lessons learned, and resources for institutions adopting HPC Carpentry workshops.

Name: marimo
Description: Programmable notebook environment with reactive, live-updating notebooks for data exploration and collaboration. Improved reproducibility over traditional formats.

Name: Google AI Professional Certificate
Description: Google's online professional certificate covering foundational AI and ML concepts - prompt engineering, LLMs, and practical AI applications.

Name: TACC Undergraduate & Graduate Education
Description: Texas Advanced Computing Center's education programs for undergraduate and graduate students. Includes REU programs, training workshops, graduate fellowships, and hands-on research experiences.

Name: SC (Supercomputing Conference)
Description: Premier international conference for HPC, data analytics, and supercomputing. Largest gathering of HPC professionals worldwide with technical sessions, exhibitions, and networking.

Name: PEARC (Practical and Extreme Computing Conference)
Description: Flagship conference for research computing professionals and students. Offers tutorials, short courses, and student travel support.

Name: HPDC (High-Performance Parallel Computing)
Description: ACM International Symposium on High-Performance Parallel and Distributed Computing. Leading venue for cutting-edge research in parallel computing systems and algorithms.

Name: IEEE IPDPS (International Parallel and Distributed Processing Symposium)
Description: IEEE's premier conference on parallel and distributed processing. Features research on distributed computing, HPC systems, and applications.

Name: SIAM CSI (Computing in Science and Engineering)
Description: SIAM conference showcasing computational research across engineering, science, and mathematics. Bridges theory and practice in computational methods.

Name: SIAM PP (Parallel Processing for Scientific Computing)
Description: SIAM conference focused on parallel computing for scientific computing. Brings together researchers developing parallel algorithms and applications.

Name: IEEE Cluster Computing
Description: IEEE International Conference on Cluster Computing. Covers cluster computing architectures, middleware, algorithms, and applications for scientific and commercial use.

Name: ISC High Performance
Description: International conference and exhibition for high-performance computing, data analytics, and networking. Major European HPC conference with global participation.

Name: HPC Campus
Description: Educational conferences focused on HPC education and outreach. Features hands-on workshops, tutorials, and networking for students and educators in HPC.

Name: HPCWire
Description: Leading publication covering the high-performance computing industry, news, insights, and thought leadership.

Name: NSF REU Sites
Description: NSF Research Experiences for Undergraduates program listing undergraduate research internships at US institutions.

Name: NSF REU Search Tool
Description: Advanced search tool to find NSF-funded REU sites beyond the main listing page. Filter by state, institution type, discipline, and funding source.

Name: Google Summer of Code (GSoC)
Description: Google's program for undergraduate students worldwide to contribute to open source development with mentorship.

Name: NASA SEED
Description: NASA Space Engineering Research and Design program for undergraduate and graduate students on real NASA missions.

Name: Anvil Supercomputer
Description: Purdue Research Computing Access (RCAC)'s high-performance computing facility. Features peta-scale computing, data storage, and visualization resources for research across all disciplines.

Name: PATh/Open Science Pool
Description: Open Science Pool provides a massive, shared pool of computing resources for research and education. PATh connects researchers to these distributed computing resources across academic and cloud environments.

Name: SULI Program (Science Undergraduate Laboratory Internships)
Description: A Department of Energy (DOE) program that offers hands-on research internship opportunities for undergraduates at national laboratories.

Name: Open Quantum Initiative (OQI) Undergraduate Fellowship
Description: A 10-week, fully funded summer research opportunity placing students at quantum research sites across the Midwest, including a $7,000 stipend and travel coverage.

Name: Q-Ready Program
Description: An online program that helps students and postdocs explore quantum careers, prepare for career paths, and meet scientists hiring for these roles.

Name: Chicago Quantum Exchange Job Board
Description: A dedicated job board to browse and sign up for alerts regarding entry-level jobs and internships in quantum computing, sensing, and networks.

Name: Quantum Education Landscape (RIT)
Description: An overview of programs, courses, minors, and majors focused on quantum information science and engineering at various institutions.

Name: Quantum Learning Resources
Description: Do-it-yourself educational resources to practice and learn quantum coding using kits like IBM's Qiskit and Xanadu's Pennylane.

Name: SGX3 Fall & Spring Training
Description: Upcoming advanced computational training sessions (Fall runs Oct-Dec, Wednesday nights) offering a $500 stipend to students, continuing the education from the summer Coding Institute.

Name: TACC Analysis Portal (TAP)
Description: The portal used to access the Texas Advanced Computing Center (TACC) at the University of Texas at Austin to launch Jupyter Notebooks on the Frontera supercomputer

Name: NAIRR Pilot Portal
Description: The National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource pilot portal managed by SGX3 for the NSF to share computing and AI resources across the research community

Name: NCAR Internships
Description: High-performance computing and data science internship opportunities at the National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR)

Name: ACCESS Allocations
Description: Portals for requesting open-source computational resources provided by the NSF

Name: Cloudbank ACCESS
Description: A computational resource allocation service provided by the ACCESS program.

Name: Benjamin Gilman Scholarship
Description: A scholarship program (provided by the U.S. Department of State) providing funding for students engaging in study abroad opportunities

Name: NCAR Explorer Series
Description: Public lectures and educational outreach materials published by NCAR.

Name: NCAR PI-WRF Teaching Box
Description: Educational resources and teaching boxes provided by NCAR for weather modeling.

Name: WeTeachCS
Description: An educational program run by TACC designed to prepare K-12 teachers for computer science certifications
